candidates.name: Lambda Cold Start Inspector
candidates.pitch: When viewing AWS Lambda in the browser console, the extension automatically parses recent cold start logs and overlays a histogram showing latency distribution per function. Uses the AWS Console's exi
candidates.pain: Serverless developers manually correlate CloudWatch logs to understand cold start patterns, which is tedious and doesn't scale across functions.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Webhook Event Playground
candidates.pitch: Intercepts and displays webhook payloads in a devtools panel, then lets developers modify and replay them directly to their local endpoint. Shows response status, timing, and body. Can save payloads a
candidates.pain: Developers debugging webhook handlers have to trigger events from production systems or use postman-style tools, which is slow and risky.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Stack Overflow Vote Momentum
candidates.pitch: The extension fetches the timeline API for any SO question to show a trend arrow—rising, falling, or stable vote counts on answers. Allows developers to filter to answers that are currently gaining tr
candidates.pain: Developers waste time reading outdated Stack Overflow answers that were once popular but are now known to be wrong or outdated.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: PR Thread Unfloader
candidates.pitch: Automatically clicks all 'load more' buttons on PR pages and expands collapsed threads using MutationObserver. Also adds a 'collapse resolved' button and a scroll-to-unread feature. Pure frontend exte
candidates.pain: GitHub PRs with many review threads require repeatedly clicking 'load more comments', slowing down code review significantly.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: Deal Room Pulse
candidates.pitch: Uses link tracking pixels injected into shared URLs. When a prospect opens a link, the extension pings a simple backend that sends a Slack/email alert. Buildable with a lightweight Go/Node backend and
candidates.pain: Sales reps have no idea when prospects are actually viewing shared proposals or contracts, missing the prime moment to follow up.

candidates.name: Gong Summary Forwarder
candidates.pitch: When a user visits Gong's call detail page, the extension detects the summary text and shows a floating action button to send it to a configured destination (Notion page, Linear issue, Slack channel).
candidates.pain: Sales reps manually copy Gong call summaries into Notion, Linear, or CRM fields, creating busywork that causes notes to get skipped.

candidates.name: CSS Origin Cascade Tracer
candidates.pitch: Extends DevTools by adding a sourcemap-aware panel that shows the full cascade with file names and line numbers for any selected element. Works by parsing CSS sourcemaps and correlating them with matc
candidates.pain: Debugging unexpected CSS behavior requires manually tracing through DevTools computed styles, which doesn't show which file or line is responsible.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: Schema Property Suggester
candidates.pitch: Scrapes the visible page content and runs it through a local heuristics engine (or lightweight API) to suggest missing structured data properties based on Schema.org vocabularies. Shows diff between c
candidates.pain: SEO developers and content creators don't know which Schema.org properties they're missing for their specific page type.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: Heap Snapshot Diff Viewer
candidates.pitch: The extension adds a button to DevTools to export heap snapshots as JSON, then provides a diff view highlighting objects that grew or appeared between snapshots. Includes filtering by object type and
candidates.pain: Finding memory leaks requires taking two heap snapshots in DevTools and manually comparing retained sizes, which is error-prone and hard to share.
